arts.ttl: Serie A | Atalanta v Como: Probable starting lineups
arts.sum: Atalanta’s recent Champions League draw against Arsenal has left a bittersweet taste, but it has also energized the team for their upcoming Serie A match against Como. While transitioning from the international stage to facing a newly promoted team might be challenging, Como’s high ambitions and the derby atmosphere will help Gasperini’s men refocus. Fabregas, Como’s manager, has clarified that he won’t be playing on the backfoot, as his team seeks their first victory of the season in Bergamo. arts.ttl: Atalanta vs. Como closes out matchday 5
arts.sum: Como have not won any of their four games in Serie A this season (D2 L2), conceding seven goals; Only Venezia and Atalanta have conceded more (8 each). Furthermore, the team from Bergamo have not drawn a match in the top-flight since April 15th at home to Hellas Verona (2-2) and are the team currently on the longest streak without a draw in the competition (11).

arts.ttl: Fabregas: “I’ve been Impressed by Sergi Roberto and Perrone. 5-4-1? Not my style”
arts.sum: Como manager Cesc Fabregas addressed the media ahead of his team’s upcoming match against Atalanta. The former Arsenal and Barcelona midfielder shared his thoughts on various topics, including the opposition, his team’s tactics, and player performances. Fabregas began by discussing Atalanta’s recent Champions League match: “I went to watch the game, which was very interesting to get some insights. Both teams have mechanisms that work from memory. We’ll need to be very alert and focused to get the three points.” When asked about his team’s condition, Fabregas provided an update: “We’re doing well. arts.ttl: Serie A | Atalanta v Como: Match Preview
arts.sum: The Gewiss Stadium in Bergamo is set to host an intriguing Serie A clash on Monday evening as Atalanta BC welcome newly-promoted Como. Fresh off their midweek Champions League exploits, Gian Piero Gasperini’s men will be eager to continue their positive momentum in domestic competition. Meanwhile, Como are still searching for their first win of the season and face a daunting challenge against one of Italy’s most dynamic sides. Atalanta enter this fixture in buoyant spirits following a hard-fought 0-0 draw against Arsenal in their Champions League opener.
